purpose: Compare style candidate families before any source, SVG, accepted-state, release-state, export, or golden mutation is authorized.
forbidden_semantic_changes: change A/B/C/D/E/F panel roles or move evidence between columns
rename symbols, measured quantities, force families, or apparatus labels
swap shallow/deep trap color semantics or force-direction meaning
remove required labels, spokes, apparatus elements, or theory-guarded mechanisms
use SVG polish to repair scientific, semantic, or label-target errors that belong in TikZ/source review
benchmark_measurable_checks: style_lock_typography must not add local tiny/scriptsize/huge overrides
visual_clash_delta should decrease_or_equal when rendered artifacts exist
human_only_questions: Does the candidate improve journal fit enough to justify leaving the current restrained manuscript style?
Does the candidate feel editorial rather than generic template polish?
Is this figure being promoted toward a flagship visual, or should it remain a restrained main-text overview?
candidate_rejection_rules: reject a prettier candidate if it changes semantic meaning, panel roles, labels, or color meaning
reject SVG polish if it is being used to fix source-level semantic defects
reject broad redesign if the only improvement is non-measurable taste language
reject any route that implies accepted-state, release-state, final-artifact, export, or golden mutation without a separate explicit gate

comparison_basis: human decision selected keep_current_style as policy state only
current style remains the benchmark until a candidate beats it on measurable checks and human art direction
failure_modes: can still be displaced by a later candidate with stronger evidence
does not approve release, accepted-state mutation, export mutation, or golden mutation
prerequisite_evidence: Wave E decision record keeps current style with no_source_mutation boundary
Wave C benchmark pack defines target style class and rejection rules

comparison_basis: candidate may improve typography, whitespace, stroke rhythm, or component fidelity
candidate must improve at least one measurable check while preserving all forbidden semantics
failure_modes: reject if it changes mechanism meaning while looking cleaner
reject if it only changes taste language without measurable or named aesthetic benefit
prerequisite_evidence: fresh comparison packet after candidate patch exists

comparison_basis: candidate could be visually stronger but must beat current style without increasing semantic or release risk
human must explicitly ask for broader visual-language alternatives after seeing benchmark tradeoffs
failure_modes: reject a prettier candidate if it changes semantic meaning
reject if it weakens panel roles, label targets, semantic colors, or mechanism story
prerequisite_evidence: human request for editorial redesign benchmark
semantic preservation evidence against all forbidden changes

comparison_basis: SVG polish is only an optical finish route after TikZ/source semantics are locked
semantic or label-target defects must return to TikZ/source review
failure_modes: reject if SVG polish is used to repair scientific, semantic, or label-target defects
reject if no positive ready_for_svg_polish evidence exists
prerequisite_evidence: ready_for_svg_polish evidence required before SVG artifact handoff
separate SVG artifact mutation approval required even after positive evidence
